message.content:
You are confirmer #1, independent. Confirm this fix is BOTH appropriate (correct, no regression, idiomatic) AND complete (the named test DISCRIMINATES — fails if reverted). Read the actual changed file + test at /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay.
Fix J-006 in .github/workflows/ci.yml
What: license gate scans the dependency set, not entire site-packages (dropped --with-system).
Test(s): test_ci_license_gate_scopes_to_dependency_set
Verdict CONFIRMED or CONCERN; be specific.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.content:
2077189 fix(hardening-p7): 6 findings incl. 2 highs (J-001 fix-regression of CV-O-1) + J-002 PII
dbb7d5c docs(hardening): pass-6 merged (#167); open pass-7 (coverage matrix + fix-regression audit)
698fb9b Merge pull request #167 from thehipsterciso/harden/pass-6
0122f77 docs(hardening): record pass-6 — convergence call CORRECTED (found a HIGH it missed)
e3eb708 fix(hardening-p6): 3 fresh findings, incl. a HIGH the convergence call missed (CV-O-1,M-1,S-1)
a8258a8 docs(hardening): pass-5 merged (#166), convergence declared; open pass-6
b960e0b Merge pull request #166 from thehipsterciso/harden/pass-5
6b5e414 docs(hardening): record pass-5 + declare CONVERGENCE (19/19 confirmed, 0 concerns)
011942b fix(hardening-p5): batch B — reliability, CI, observability, docs (13 findings)
a9f3a48 fix(hardening-p5): batch A — methodology integrity / pre-registration firewall (H-001,002,003,015,016)
---
diff --git a/.github/workflows/ci.yml b/.github/workflows/ci.yml
index 91309f3..9e1e25c 100644
--- a/.github/workflows/ci.yml
+++ b/.github/workflows/ci.yml
@@ -5,6 +5,12 @@ on:
     branches: [main]
   pull_request:
 
+# Least privilege (#H-008): the default GITHUB_TOKEN can be repo-write; this workflow only reads
+# the code and uploads artifacts, so pin the token to read-only at the top level. Any job needing
+# more must opt in explicitly with its own `permissions:`.
+permissions:
+  contents: read
+
 jobs:
   # Dependency-free core: the engine must lint, type-check, and test with NO extras installed
   # (optional backends are import-guarded; integration tests skip). This guards ADR-0006.
@@ -26,7 +32,8 @@ jobs:
           # core lock carries only the `dev` extra (no backends), preserving the ADR-0006 guard.
           python -m pip install --upgrade pip
           pip install --require-hashes -r requirements-core.lock
-          pip install --no-deps -e .
+          pip install --require-hashes -r requirements-build.lock  # hashed build backend (#J-003)
+          pip install --no-build-isolation --no-deps -e .
       - name: Lint (ruff)
         run: ruff check src tests
       - name: Format check (ruff)
@@ -71,9 +78,26 @@ jobs:
           # release within a pyproject range could run during the real-backend tests (#F-004).
           python -m pip install --upgrade pip
           pip install --require-hashes -r requirements.lock
-          pip install --no-deps -e .
+          pip install --require-hashes -r requirements-build.lock  # hashed build backend (#J-003)
+          pip install --no-build-isolation --no-deps -e .
       - name: Type-check against real backend types (mypy)
         run: mypy
+      - name: Verify service containers are reachable (no silent skip)
+        # #H-009: the live Postgres/Qdrant tests SKIP when the service is unreachable, so a
+        # misconfigured/never-started container would let the integration job report GREEN without
+        # actually exercising the backends. Fail loudly here if either service isn't up, so the
+        # gate cannot pass vacuously.
+        run: |
+          set -euo pipefail
+          for i in $(seq 1 30); do
+            pg_isready -h localhost -p 5432 -U postgres && break || sleep 2
+            [ "$i" = "30" ] && { echo "::error::Postgres service never became ready"; exit 1; }
+          done
+          for i in $(seq 1 30); do
+            curl -fsS http://localhost:6333/readyz && break || sleep 2
+            [ "$i" = "30" ] && { echo "::error::Qdrant service never became ready"; exit 1; }
+          done
+          echo "postgres + qdrant reachable"
       # Real backends now run: langgraph graph + interrupt/resume, mlflow lifecycle, OTel,
       # numpy primitives, the T3 drain/classification (synthetic SDK, no metered call), AND —
       # via the service containers — the live Postgres checkpointer + Qdrant vector store.
@@ -94,7 +118,11 @@ jobs:
         with:
           python-version: "3.12"
       - name: Install pip-audit
-        run: python -m pip install --upgrade pip pip-audit
+        # Pin the audit tooling to exact versions (#G-022): an unpinned pip-audit can change
+        # advisory results / exit behavior between releases, making the supply-chain gate
+        # non-reproducible (a green commit could fail tomorrow on a tooling bump) — the analogue
+        # of the uv pin (#F-031).
+        run: python -m pip install --upgrade pip && python -m pip install pip-audit==2.10.1
       - name: Lockfiles are in sync with pyproject (reproducibility guard)
         run: |
           # Pin uv to the exact version the lockfiles were generated with (#F-031): an unpinned
@@ -110,6 +138,12 @@ jobs:
           uv pip compile pyproject.toml --extra dev --universal --generate-hashes -o /tmp/requirements-core.regen.lock
           diff <(grep -v '^#' requirements-core.lock) <(grep -v '^#' /tmp/requirements-core.regen.lock) \
             || { echo "::error::requirements-core.lock is stale — run: uv pip compile pyproject.toml --extra dev --universal --generate-hashes -o requirements-core.lock"; exit 1; }
+          # the build-backend lockfile (hatchling etc.) backs the hash-pinned --no-build-isolation
+          # editable install (#J-003) and must stay in sync.
+          printf 'hatchling\neditables\n' > /tmp/build-reqs.in
+          uv pip compile /tmp/build-reqs.in --universal --generate-hashes -o /tmp/requirements-build.regen.lock
+          diff <(grep -v '^#' requirements-build.lock) <(grep -v '^#' /tmp/requirements-build.regen.lock) \
+            || { echo "::error::requirements-build.lock is stale — run: uv pip compile <(printf 'hatchling\\neditables\\n') --universal --generate-hashes -o requirements-build.lock"; exit 1; }
       - name: pip-audit (reproducible known-vulnerability scan)
         run: |
           set -euo pipefail
@@ -132,7 +166,19 @@ jobs:
           # downstream license/transitive monitoring has a stable artifact to track (#147). No
           # `|| true` mask (#F-012): a pip-audit failure must fail the step, and the SBOM must be
           # non-empty, so a broken/absent SBOM cannot pass CI green.
-          pip-audit --progress-spinner off -r requirements.lock --format cyclonedx-json -o sbom.cyclonedx.json
+          # Apply the SAME allowlist as the gate (#G-015): pip-audit exits non-zero on ANY finding
+          # regardless of output format, so without the allowlist an ACCEPTED advisory would fail
+          # SBOM generation here even though the gate step passed — coupling SBOM emission to vuln
+          # status and making the allowlist ineffective. The gate step already ran first, so a
+          # NON-allowlisted advisory has failed the job before we reach here.
+          IGNORES=()
+          if [ -f .pip-audit-ignore ]; then
+            while IFS= read -r id || [ -n "$id" ]; do
+              case "$id" in ''|\#*) continue ;; esac
+              IGNORES+=(--ignore-vuln "$id")
+            done < .pip-audit-ignore
+          fi
+          pip-audit --progress-spinner off -r requirements.lock --format cyclonedx-json -o sbom.cyclonedx.json "${IGNORES[@]}"
           test -s sbom.cyclonedx.json

341	    # tag can be force-pushed to malicious code). A version tag is allowed only as a comment.

content.text:
Verification complete.

Findings:
- The fix is in `/Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/.github/workflows/ci.yml` line 202: `pip-licenses --format=json > licenses.json` — `--with-system` has been dropped. By default pip-licenses scopes to the installed environment's distributions; the surrounding step installs only `requirements.lock` + the build backend + the editable package (`--no-deps`), so the scan covers exactly the pinned dependency set, not unrelated system/runner tooling. This is correct and idiomatic, and consistent with the J-006 comment and the F-013 lockfile-scoping rationale.
- No regression: `--with-system` appears nowhere in the workflow; the license gate still runs `scripts/license_gate.py licenses.json` afterward.
- The test `test_ci_license_gate_scopes_to_dependency_set` in `/Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/tests/test_supply_chain.py:330` asserts the exact `--with-system` invocation string is absent. It passes on the current tree and FAILS when I reinjected `--with-system` (then restored), so it genuinely discriminates.

One minor note (not a concern): the test asserts the specific substring `pip-licenses --format=json --with-system`; a revert using a different flag ordering would technically slip past. But the original bug was exactly this form, so it discriminates against the real regression.

input.reasoning: Fix at .github/workflows/ci.yml:202 drops --with-system: `pip-licenses --format=json > licenses.json`. pip-licenses now defaults to scoping the scan to the installed distributions, and the step installs only requirements.lock + the hashed build backend + the editable package (--no-deps), so the license gate covers exactly the pinned dependency set rather than the runner's system tooling — correct, idiomatic, aligned with the J-006/F-013 rationale. No --with-system remains anywhere in the workflow; the gate still invokes scripts/license_gate.py licenses.json, so no regression. The test tests/test_supply_chain.py:330 (test_ci_license_gate_scopes_to_dependency_set) reads the CI YAML and asserts the --with-system invocation is absent. It passes on the current tree; I reinjected --with-system via sed and the test FAILED (AssertionError at line 334), then restored the file (git diff clean). The test genuinely discriminates against the real regression. Minor non-blocking note: the assertion matches a fixed substring including flag order, so a differently-ordered revert could slip past — but that is not the original bug shape.

content.text: CONFIRMED. The J-006 fix in `/Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/.github/workflows/ci.yml:202` correctly drops `--with-system`, scoping the license gate to the installed dependency set, and the test `test_ci_license_gate_scopes_to_dependency_set` discriminates (passes now, fails when reverted).
